product_name: Pip and Posy: The New Friend
product_description_under_30_words: At the beach, Posy wakes to find Pip playing with new friend Zac — jealousy, then a kind act reunites them.
ember_verdict: Not another feelings chart — this is the moment your child watches their best friend laugh with someone else. Posy fixes it herself, which gives you language for real playgroup wobbles.
personalization_hint: likely to be upset when a close friend suddenly prefers another playmate
why_it_fits: Shows jealousy, inclusion and repair in peer play without adult rescue — the core 34–36m shift beyond emotion labelling.
caveats: Paperback ISBN 9780857638748; board book is a different ISBN. Use Ages 2–5 interest, not Nosy’s board-book 0–2 tag alone.
gift_note: Warm story gift after a messy playdate — not a lecture wrapped in paper.
ownership_note: Borrow first if the shelf already has several Pip & Posy or Bright/Field titles.
review_quality_note: Goodreads ~3.9; Amazon storefront 4.9/396 used to meet FF rating gate.
preloved_signal_note: Picture books hold up well second-hand.
substitute_if_unavailable: Pip and Posy: The Super Scooter

product_description_under_30_words: Llama shares blocks until Nelly takes Fuzzy Llama — tears, a torn toy, then Mama helps them try again.
ember_verdict: Playdates go wrong over the one special toy. This names the snatch, the meltdown and the restart without pretending sharing is easy on demand.
personalization_hint: likely to share most toys but freeze when a friend grabs a favourite comforter
why_it_fits: Concrete sharing conflict with a friend in the home — turn-taking and repair, not emotion jars.
caveats: Hardback is pricier; borrow from library first if budget is tight. Waterstones hardback stock can run thin.
gift_note: Warm story gift after a messy playdate — not a lecture wrapped in paper.
ownership_note: Borrow first if the shelf already has several Pip & Posy or Bright/Field titles.
review_quality_note: Goodreads ~4.25; Amazon 4.8 with 2,800+ ratings used for FF gate.
preloved_signal_note: Picture books hold up well second-hand.
substitute_if_unavailable: How Do Dinosaurs Play with Their Friends?

product_name: Pip and Posy: The Super Scooter
product_description_under_30_words: Posy borrows Pip’s scooter without asking, falls off, says sorry — then they rebuild the play with a plaster and sandcastle.
ember_verdict: The nearly-three “I wanted a turn” story in miniature: snatching, a bump, sorry, and friendship still intact. Different beat from The New Friend’s jealousy arc.
personalization_hint: likely to grab wheeled toys from friends and need a simple sorry script afterwards
why_it_fits: Turn-taking, permission and apology between friends — practical social repair for this cluster.
caveats: Same series as pick 1 — fine as a pair, skip if the shelf already has three Pip & Posy titles.
gift_note: Warm story gift after a messy playdate — not a lecture wrapped in paper.
ownership_note: Borrow first if the shelf already has several Pip & Posy or Bright/Field titles.
review_quality_note: Goodreads ~4.06; Amazon 4.7/579 used for FF gate. UK Amazon editions often ~4.8.
preloved_signal_note: Picture books hold up well second-hand.
substitute_if_unavailable: Pip and Posy: The New Friend

product_description_under_30_words: Two greedy squirrels race for the last pine cone until teamwork — and a shared laugh — saves them.
ember_verdict: For the child who turns every turn into a race. Bright/Field make sharing feel like adventure, not a lecture — useful after nursery toy rows.
personalization_hint: likely to compete hard for the “best” toy and need help noticing a friend still matters more
why_it_fits: Competition, sharing and friendship repair in one short rhyme — matches turn-taking without Colour Monster labelling.
caveats: Rhyming text is denser than Pip & Posy; some reads land better at the top of the band. Ages 3–4 overlaps month 36.
gift_note: Warm story gift after a messy playdate — not a lecture wrapped in paper.
ownership_note: Borrow first if the shelf already has several Pip & Posy or Bright/Field titles.
review_quality_note: Goodreads ~4.28; Amazon storefronts commonly 4.8 with thousands of ratings.
preloved_signal_note: Picture books hold up well second-hand.

product_name: How Do Dinosaurs Play with Their Friends?
product_description_under_30_words: Rhyming dinos model what not to do with friends — hiding toys, pouty play — then how to share and take turns.
ember_verdict: Short, shouty and useful after a rough playdate. Less story than Pip & Posy, more of a “shall we try again?” reset for nearly-threes who already know the rules and still break them.
personalization_hint: likely to refuse turns and need a funny mirror of the behaviour before trying again
why_it_fits: Explicit friend-play manners and sharing — complements narrative picks with a quick board-book script.
caveats: Board book is brief; some parents find the “how not to” setup repetitive. Ages 3–5 overlaps month 36.
gift_note: Warm story gift after a messy playdate — not a lecture wrapped in paper.
ownership_note: Borrow first if the shelf already has several Pip & Posy or Bright/Field titles.
review_quality_note: Goodreads ~4.14; Amazon 4.9/2,400+ used for FF gate.
preloved_signal_note: Picture books hold up well second-hand.

product_name: Should I Share My Ice Cream?
why_it_fits: Gerald overthinks sharing ice cream with Piggie — funny friendship dilemma.
caveats: Strong ratings, but publisher reading age often 4–8 — hold as backup not Top Pick for 34–36m gate.

product_name: Pip and Posy: The Friendly Snail
why_it_fits: Noise vs quiet play — friends clash, then rescue each other.
Two creatures fight over a chair shouting mine — then choose play instead.
caveats: Perfect theme but Goodreads ~4.02 fails FF 4.4 gate — backup only.

why_it_fits: Kite accident sparks HORRIBLE BEAR — mutual mistakes lead to sorry.
caveats: Apology theme strong; ratings hover ~4.0 on Goodreads — backup.

why_it_fits: Hermit crab shares a shell with friends — crowding, kindness, belonging.
caveats: Beloved Donaldson; more metaphor/space-sharing than peer toy rows.

why_it_fits: Three frogs squabble over a pond until a storm forces sharing.
caveats: Mother & Baby staple; classic tone; ratings often sit under 4.4.

why_it_fits: Little Nutbrown Hare meets Tipps and asks the friendship question.
caveats: Gentle first-friendship; Goodreads ~3.8 fails rating gate.

why_it_fits: Toddler monopolises toys at childcare, then offers one to reconnect.
caveats: Perfect age overlap (Ages 1–4) but ratings too low for Top Pick.

why_it_fits: Little Whale hates sharing pod, snacks and Mum until Blue models sharing.
caveats: Publisher page live; Goodreads 3.51 — demoted from prior Top Pick.

why_it_fits: Tiny Hugasaurus learns hugs do not fix every playground row.
caveats: Friendship kindness backup; less specific than Top 5 conflict plots.

age_mark_on_listing: Ages 3 and up (Simon & Schuster); classroom guides often 5–7
skip_reason: Theme fits (three-way friendship) but FF age gate fails: publisher Ages 3+ (min 36m excludes 34–35m) and Reading Realm/classroom guides pitch 5–7. Demoted from prior Top Pick per founder hard gate.
evidence_notes: Verified Simon & Schuster UK Ages 3 and up; Scholastic 3–6; Reading Realm “perfect for 5–7”.

skip_reason: Publisher/reading age 4–8 does not overlap 34–36m under FF range gate. Demoted from prior Top Pick.
Basic colour-coded emotion labelling — right for 28–30m, not friendship conflict at nearly three. Explicit founder exclusion from fresh Top Picks.
Publisher min age 4+ sits above the 34–36m band for default Top Picks; better as a later Big Bright Feelings title.
Parent–child separation comfort — not peer friendship, sharing or turn-taking.
Friendship theme is strong but publisher age 4–7 / reading age 4–8 does not overlap 34–36m.

ember_verdict: If you already own two or more Pip & Posy titles, borrow The New Friend or Super Scooter from the library before buying another. The friendship beats are close cousins.

product_description_under_30_words: Bring back out after a rough playdate
ember_verdict: These earn their keep the evening after a toy row or left-out wobble — one short read, then one sentence of your own (“remember Posy?”), not a lecture.

product_description_under_30_words: Hold off on Colour Monster and 4–8 reading-age titles
ember_verdict: Emotion-labelling classics and early-reader friendship titles can wait. This band wants peer conflict and repair language that matches nearly-three play.
